reasoning: The 2025 MLS season will be San Diego FC's inaugural year. While recent MLS expansion teams have had mixed results (Atlanta, LAFC, Nashville, St. Louis made it; Minnesota, Cincinnati, Austin, Charlotte didn't), 9 out of 15 teams in each conference currently make the playoffs, establishing a high base rate (60%) for an average team. San Diego FC is building a solid roster with their Right to Dream pipeline and DP Hirving 'Chucky' Lozano, but their head coach, Mikey Varas, has limited club experience. Factoring in the typical challenges of building team chemistry from scratch in an inaugural season, I estimate they will perform slightly below the league average in their first year.
